nativeRange.habitat: Generally found in sandy and rubble areas of coral reefs in relatively shallow water where the substrate is suitable for burrow construction.

decorPreferences: deep sand bed with mixed rubble for burrow construction
stable rockwork (rocks set directly on the tank bottom, not on sand, to prevent collapse from burrowing)

diet.notes: Generally a scavenging carnivore — has been documented taking meaty foods such as frozen mysis and brine shrimp, finely chopped seafood, sinking pellets, and bottom-feeder tablets. In a goby-paired setup, food sharing with the partner goby is commonly observed; spot-feeding near the burrow entrance is often advised.

goodWith: shrimp gobies (Stonogobiops yasha, S. nematodes, Amblyeleotris spp., Cryptocentrus spp.)
most LPS, SPS, and soft corals
avoidWith: other pistol or snapping shrimp (conspecific aggression)
ornamental shrimp small enough to be prey (sexy shrimp, anemone shrimp)
large predatory fish (triggers, large wrasses, hawkfish, lionfish)

imageCandidates.notes: Dan Schofield, 2048x1535. Sharp, vibrant in-situ shot showing the diagnostic bold red-and-white banded body alongside a partner shrimp goby at the shared burrow — best illustrates the species' coloration and its signature goby symbiosis.

Hkchan123 (Chinese Wikipedia), 592x382. Crisp full-body dorsal view of a lone specimen with the whole animal and enlarged claws in frame, clearly showing the red-and-white candy-stripe banding; modest resolution.
Hkchan123 (Chinese Wikipedia), 566x381. Clean lateral profile of the same-type specimen giving a different angle on the red-and-white banding; modest resolution.

summary: A small, striped Indo-Pacific pistol shrimp commonly paired with shrimp gobies (notably the Yasha goby) in burrow-sharing symbioses. Generally considered reef-safe and beginner-friendly given a deep sand bed and rubble.
careNotes: Randall's Pistol Shrimp (Alpheus randalli) is among the more sought-after alpheid shrimp in the marine hobby, prized for its bold red-and-white banded coloration and for its near-obligate burrow-sharing symbiosis with small shrimp gobies. In the wild it co-inhabits a sand-and-rubble burrow with a partner goby (commonly Stonogobiops yasha, S. nematodes, or various Amblyeleotris and Cryptocentrus species); the shrimp excavates and maintains the burrow while the goby acts as a sentinel, signalling threats with tail flicks. Pairing in captivity generally succeeds within a week or two when the two species are introduced together. The setup should provide a sand bed of at least 5 cm, ideally deeper, mixed with small rubble for burrow construction, with rockwork resting on the bare bottom rather than on the sand so that excavation cannot destabilise the aquascape. A tank of at least 75 L (roughly 20 US gal) gives sufficient space for a goby partner and helps maintain stable water parameters. Like other alpheid shrimp, Randall's Pistol Shrimp is generally intolerant of copper-based medications, and adequate iodine and trace-element levels are commonly advised to support healthy molting. The loud snap from its enlarged claw is harmless to tank inhabitants but can be startling; it is occasionally implicated in damaging thin glass on very small tanks, though such reports are rare. Avoid housing with other pistol or snapping shrimp, and with delicate ornamental shrimp small enough to be taken as prey.
breedingNotes: Captive breeding is rarely reported and is not commercially established. Like most alpheids the species is believed to be a pair-bonded burrow dweller in the wild, with planktonic larval stages that are difficult to rear in standard aquaria.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Listed as 'Red Banded Snapping Shrimp (Alpheus randalli)'. Source of headline care parameters: 72-78°F, pH 8.1-8.4, dKH 8-12, SG 1.023-1.025, ~2" max size, peaceful, reef safe, carnivore.

additional.name: BlueZoo Aquatics
additional.notes: Assigned secondary returned 404 / no species page at the product URL. Cross-verification fell through to other trade sources listed below.

additional.name: Reeflings — Alpheus randalli Complete Care Guide
additional.notes: Cross-checked sizing (5 cm / 2 in), temperature (24-26°C), pH (8.0-8.5), SG (1.023-1.026), 50 L minimum tank, Indo-Pacific range, sand-and-rubble reef habitat, peaceful temperament, reef-safe status, and the goby symbiosis (Amblyeleotris).

additional.name: Saltwaterfish.com — Pistol Shrimp Randalli
additional.notes: Cross-checked max size 2 in, 10-gal minimum (lower end of the trade-source spread), reef safe, peaceful, and goby symbiosis behavior.

additional.name: Aquatics Unlimited — Candy Pistol Shrimp (Alpheus randalli)
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (76-78°F), pH (8.1-8.4), dKH (8-12), SG (1.024-1.026), 15-gal minimum, deep sand for burrowing, Indo-Pacific origin.

crustacean.moltingNotes: No established molt interval has been reported for this species in captivity. Adequate iodine and trace-element levels are commonly advised to support successful molts; shed exoskeletons are generally left in the burrow and reabsorbed for calcium.
crustacean.breedingNotes: Not commercially bred in the hobby; planktonic larvae make rearing impractical in standard reef aquaria.
